speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: The gray-eyed morn smiles on the frowning night,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Check’ring the eastern clouds with streaks of light,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And fleckled darkness like a drunkard reels

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: From forth day’s path and Titan’s fiery wheels.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Now, ere the sun advance his burning eye,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: The day to cheer and night’s dank dew to dry,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: I must upfill this osier cage of ours

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: With baleful weeds and precious-juicèd flowers.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: The Earth that’s nature’s mother is her tomb;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: What is her burying grave, that is her womb;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And from her womb children of divers kind

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: We sucking on her natural bosom find,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Many for many virtues excellent,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: None but for some, and yet all different.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: O, mickle is the powerful grace that lies

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: In plants, herbs, stones, and their true qualities.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: For naught so vile that on the Earth doth live

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: But to the Earth some special good doth give;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Nor aught so good but, strained from that fair use,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Revolts from true birth, stumbling on abuse.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Virtue itself turns vice, being misapplied,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And vice sometime by action dignified.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Within the infant rind of this weak flower

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Poison hath residence and medicine power:

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: For this, being smelt, with that part cheers each part;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Being tasted, stays all senses with the heart.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Two such opposèd kings encamp them still

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: In man as well as herbs—grace and rude will;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And where the worser is predominant,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Full soon the canker death eats up that plant.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Good morrow, father.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Benedicite.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: What early tongue so sweet saluteth me?

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Young son, it argues a distempered head

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: So soon to bid “Good morrow” to thy bed.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Care keeps his watch in every old man’s eye,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And, where care lodges, sleep will never lie;

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: But where unbruisèd youth with unstuffed brain

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Doth couch his limbs, there golden sleep doth reign.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Therefore thy earliness doth me assure

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thou art uproused with some distemp’rature,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Or, if not so, then here I hit it right:

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Our Romeo hath not been in bed tonight.

speaker: ROMEO
text: That last is true. The sweeter rest was mine.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: God pardon sin! Wast thou with Rosaline?

speaker: ROMEO
text: With Rosaline, my ghostly father? No.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I have forgot that name and that name’s woe.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: That’s my good son. But where hast thou been then?

speaker: ROMEO
text: I’ll tell thee ere thou ask it me again.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I have been feasting with mine enemy,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Where on a sudden one hath wounded me

speaker: ROMEO
text: That’s by me wounded. Both our remedies

speaker: ROMEO
text: Within thy help and holy physic lies.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I bear no hatred, blessèd man, for, lo,

speaker: ROMEO
text: My intercession likewise steads my foe.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Be plain, good son, and homely in thy drift.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Riddling confession finds but riddling shrift.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Then plainly know my heart’s dear love is set

speaker: ROMEO
text: On the fair daughter of rich Capulet.

speaker: ROMEO
text: As mine on hers, so hers is set on mine,

speaker: ROMEO
text: And all combined, save what thou must combine

speaker: ROMEO
text: By holy marriage. When and where and how

speaker: ROMEO
text: We met, we wooed, and made exchange of vow

speaker: ROMEO
text: I’ll tell thee as we pass, but this I pray,

speaker: ROMEO
text: That thou consent to marry us today.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Holy Saint Francis, what a change is here!

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Is Rosaline, that thou didst love so dear,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: So soon forsaken? Young men’s love then lies

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Not truly in their hearts, but in their eyes.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Jesu Maria, what a deal of brine

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Hath washed thy sallow cheeks for Rosaline!

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: How much salt water thrown away in waste

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: To season love, that of it doth not taste!

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: The sun not yet thy sighs from heaven clears,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thy old groans yet ringing in mine ancient ears.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Lo, here upon thy cheek the stain doth sit

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Of an old tear that is not washed off yet.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: If e’er thou wast thyself, and these woes thine,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thou and these woes were all for Rosaline.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: And art thou changed? Pronounce this sentence then:

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Women may fall when there’s no strength in men.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Thou chid’st me oft for loving Rosaline.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: For doting, not for loving, pupil mine.

speaker: ROMEO
text: And bad’st me bury love.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Not in a grave

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: To lay one in, another out to have.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I pray thee, chide me not. Her I love now

speaker: ROMEO
text: Doth grace for grace and love for love allow.

speaker: ROMEO
text: The other did not so.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: O, she knew well

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Thy love did read by rote, that could not spell.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: But come, young waverer, come, go with me.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: In one respect I’ll thy assistant be,

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: For this alliance may so happy prove

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: To turn your households’ rancor to pure love.

speaker: ROMEO
text: O, let us hence. I stand on sudden haste.

speaker:
FRIAR
               
              LAWRENCE
text: Wisely and slow. They stumble that run fast.
